title: 『LPI−102』Exam test 102-500（Exam 1)
content:
1. Which of the following commands display the number of bytes transmitted and received via the eth0 network interface? (Choose two.)

  • A. route -v via eth0

  • B. ip stats show dev eth0

  • C. netstat -s -i eth0

  • D. ifconfig eth0

  • E. ip -s link show eth0

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: CD

Comment

  • Answer D + E
    netstat -s and -i do not work together. Tested with ubuntu 20.04 and centos 7.
    13

  • D and E.
    19
    1

2. FILL BLANK Which parameter is missing in the command ip link set ____ dev eth0 to activate the previously inactive network interface eth0? (Specify the parameter only without any command, path or additional options.)

  • A. up
Click to show answer

Correct Answer: A

Comment

3. Which of the commands below might have produced the following output?

  • A. dig -t mx www.example.org

  • B. dig www.example.org

  • C. dig -t ns www.example.org

  • D. dig -t a www.example.org

  • E. dig -t soa www.example.org

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: B

Comment

  • C is correct.
    Man dig
    -t type
    This option indicates the resource record type to query, which can be any valid query type. If it is a resource record type supported in BIND 9, it can be given by the type mnemonic (such as NS or AAAA). The default query type is A, unless the -x option is supplied to indicate a reverse lookup. A zone transfer can be requested by specifying a type of AXFR. When an incremental zone transfer (IXFR) is required , set the type to ixfr=N. The incremental zone transfer contains all changes made to the zone since the serial number in the zone’s SOA record was N. All resource record types can be expressed as TYPEnn, where nn is the number of the type. If the resource record type is not supported in BIND 9, the result is displayed as described in RFC 3597.

  • I tried both NS and MX and i get the similar results from both of those, but then i started to examen the picture and the results from the command and it clearly says that NS is used if you look at the picture and after:
    ; http://www.example. IN NS <—– NS was used
    So dig -t ns http://www.example.org command was used.
    1
    1

  • Does someone have a valid reasoning for the answer? because option C is the one providing the output in the picture…

  • i think the correct answer is C because there is “NS” at the end of line 8, when we enter the command in option “B” we get A at the end of line 8
    1

  • If B is the answer then D would be correct to as this is the default behavior.
    I think C is correct as the Question section replies with NS
    7

4. What output does the command seq 1 5 20 produce?

  • A. 1

  • B. 1

  • C. 1

  • D. 2

  • E. 5

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: B

Comment

  • B is right, but the Sequence is “1 6 11 16”

5. What is true about the file .profile in a user’s home directory?

  • A. It must be executable.

  • B. It must call the binary of the login shell.

  • C. It must use a valid shell script syntax.

  • D. It must start with a shebang.

  • E. It must be readable for its owner only.

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: E

Comment

  • E is incorrect.

~/.profile: executed by the command interpreter for login shells.

This file is not read by bash(1), if ~/.bash_profile or ~/.bash_login

exists.

see /usr/share/doc/bash/examples/startup-files for examples.

the files are located in the bash-doc package.

the default umask is set in /etc/profile; for setting the umask

for ssh logins, install and configure the libpam-umask package.

#umask 022

  • I think the correct answer is C.
    When opening Terminal in BASH on Ubuntu Linux, the program automatically searches for a PROFILE file and executes it line by line as a shell script.
    4

  • Em meu Centos esta 644, não seria a letra C ?
    2

6. Which of the following files is not read directly by a Bash login shell?

  • A. ~/.bashrc

  • B. ~/.bash_profile

  • C. ~/.bash_login

  • D. ~/.profile

  • E. /etc/profile

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: A

Comment

  • https://www.gnu.org/software/bash/manual/html_node/Bash-Startup-Files.html
    3

7. FILL BLANK Which command is used to sync the hardware clock to the system clock? (Specify only the command without any path or parameters.)

  • A. hwclock
Click to show answer

Correct Answer: A

Comment

  • With parameters:
    hwclock –hctosys
    or
    hwclock -s

8. Which of the following features are provided by SPICE? (Choose two.)

  • A. Connecting local USB devices to remote applications.

  • B. Accessing graphical applications on a remote host.

  • C. Replacing Xorg as local X11 server.

  • D. Downloading and locally installing applications from a remote machine.

  • E. Uploading and running a binary program on a remote machine.

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: AD

Comment

  • A B

  • A and B
    19

9. On a machine running several X servers, how do programs identify the different instances of the X11 server?

  • A. By a fixed UUID that is defined in the X11 configuration file.

  • B. By a display name like: 1.

  • C. By the name of the user that runs the X server like x11:bob.

  • D. By a device name like /dev/X11/xservers/1.

  • E. By a unique IPv6 address from the fe80::/64 subnet.

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: B

Comment

10. Given the following excerpt of the sudo configuration: jane ANY=NOPASSWD: /bin/kill, /bin/id, PASSWD: /sbin/fdisk

  • A. Jane can run /bin/id only after specifying her password.

  • B. Jane can run /sbin/fdisk after specifying root’s password.

  • C. Jane can run /sbin/fdisk after specifying her password.

  • D. Jane can run /bin/kill without specifying a password.

  • E. Jane can run /bin/id without specifying her password.

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: CDE

Comment

11. What output does the command seq 10 produce?

  • A. A continuous stream of numbers increasing in increments of 10 until the command is stopped.

  • B. It creates no output because a second parameter is missing.

  • C. The number 0 through 9 with one number per line.

  • D. The number 10 to standard output.

  • E. The numbers 1 through 10 with one number per line.

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: E

Comment

12. What output is produced by the following command sequence? echo ‘1 2 3 4 5 6’

  • A. result: 6 5 4

  • B. result: 1 2 3 4 5 6

  • C. result: 3 4 5 6 2 1

  • D. result: 6 5 4 3 2 1

  • E. result: 3 2 1

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: C

Comment

13. FILL BLANK What is the top-level directory which contains the configuration files for CUPS? (Specify the full path to the directory.)

  • A. /etc/cups/ cups-files.conf
Click to show answer

Correct Answer: A

Comment

  • The configuration files of CUPS are all located in /etc/cups .
    The question is about directory not a file.
    2
    1

  • I am not sure ,But I think [/etc/cups/cupsd.conf] is the right answer.
    2

  • top level directory.
    I think /etc/cups is enough
    14

  • is this one Answer or two different answers? (because the whitespace)
    2

14. Which option in the chrony configuration file changes the initial interval of polls to a NTP server in order to speed up the initial synchronization?

  • A. iburst

  • B. quickstart

  • C. fast

  • D. fsync

  • E. flood

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: A

Comment

15. FILL BLANK Which command included in systemd supports selecting messages from the systemd journal by criteria such as time or unit name? (Specify only the command without any path or parameters.)

  • A. journalctl
Click to show answer

Correct Answer: A

Comment

16. FILL BLANK Which file, if present, must contain all users that are allowed to use the cron scheduling system? (Specify the full name of the file, including path.)

  • A. /etc/cron.allow
Click to show answer

Correct Answer: A

Comment

17. Which of the following information is stored in /etc/shadow for each user?

  • A. The timestamp of the user’s last login

  • B. The user’s private SSH keys

  • C. The hashed password of the user

  • D. The numerical user ID (UID)

  • E. The path to the user’s home directory

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: C

Comment

18. FILL BLANK Which command included in NetworkManager is a curses application which provides easy acces to the NetworkManager on the command line? (Specify only the command without any path or parameters.)

  • A. nmtui
Click to show answer

Correct Answer: A

Comment

19. Which of the following is a valid IPv6 address?

  • A. 2001:db8:0g21::1

  • B. 2001::db8:4581::1

  • C. 2001:db8:3241::1

  • D. 2001%db8%9990%%1

  • E. 2001.db8.819f..1

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: C

Comment

20. Which of the following programs uses the hosts.allow file to perform its main task of checking for access control restrictions to system services?

  • A. tcpd

  • B. inetd

  • C. fingerd

  • D. mountd

  • E. xinetd

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: A

Comment

21. SIMULATION The presence of what file will temporarily prevent all users except root from logging into the system? (Specify the full name of the file, including path.)

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: /etc/nologin

Comment

22. On a regular users workstation the route command takes a long time before printing out the routing table. Which of the following errors does that indicate?

  • A. The local routing information may be corrupted and must be re-validated using a routing protocol.

  • B. One of the routers in the routing table is not available which causes the automatic router failure detection mechanism (ARF-D) to wait for a timeout.

  • C. There may accidentally be more than one default router in which case a default router election has to be done on the network in order to choose one router as the default.

  • D. DNS resolution may not be working as route by default tries to resolve names of routers and destinations and may run into a timeout.

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: D

Comment

23. Which of the following statements is valid in the file /etc/nsswitch.conf?

  • A. multi on B. 192.168.168.4 dns-server

  • C. hosts: files dns

  • D. include /etc/nsswitch.d/

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: C

Comment

24. SIMULATION What is the lowest numbered unprivileged TCP port? (Specify the number in digits only.)

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: 1024

Comment

  • 1024

25. Which of the following commands can be used to display the local routing table? (Choose TWO correct answers.)

  • A. ifconfig

  • B. dig

  • C. netstat

  • D. route

  • E. trackroute

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: CD

Comment

26. What is the command to delete the default gateway from the system IP routing table? (Choose TWO correct answers.)

  • A. route del default

  • B. ifconfig unset default

  • C. netstat -r default

  • D. ip route del default

  • E. sysctl ipv4.default_gw=0

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: AD

Comment

27. SIMULATION After configuring printing on a Linux server, the administrator sends a test file to one of the printers and it fails to print. What command can be used to display the status of the printer’s queue? (Specify ONLY the command without any path or parameters.)

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: lpq -or- /usr/bin/lpq -or- lpstat -or- /usr/bin/lpstat

Comment

28. SIMULATION In which file, if present, must all users be listed that are allowed to use the cron scheduling system? (Specify the full name of the file, including path.)

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: /etc/cron.allow

Comment

29. To prevent a specific user from scheduling tasks with at, what should the administrator do?

  • A. Add the specific user to /etc/at.allow file.

  • B. Add the specific user to [deny] section in the /etc/atd.conf file.

  • C. Add the specific user to /etc/at.deny file.

  • D. Add the specific user to nojobs group.

  • E. Run the following: atd –deny [user].

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: C

Comment

30. SIMULATION Which environment variable should be set in order to change the time zone for the commands run from within the environment variable’s scope?

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: TZ

Comment

31. Which of the following commands can remove a user from a group?

  • A. grouprm

  • B. groupmod

  • C. passwd

  • D. usergroups

  • E. usermod

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: E

Comment

32. SIMULATION The system’s timezone may be set by linking /etc/localtime to an appropriate file in which directory? (Provide the full path to the directory, without any country information)

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: /usr/share/zoneinfo/

Comment

33. SIMULATION What is the default name of the configuration file for the Xorg X11 server? (Specify the file name only without any path.)

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: xorg.conf

Comment

  • Reasonable chance the exam will ask the full path
    /etc/X11/xorg.conf

34. What is the purpose of a screen reader?

  • A. It reads text displayed on the screen to blind or visually impaired people.

  • B. It reads the parameters of the attached monitors and creates an appropriate X11 configuration.

  • C. It displays lines and markers to help people use speed reading techniques.

  • D. It manages and displays files that contain e-books.

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: A

Comment

35. SIMULATION Which command can be used to investigate the properties for a particular window in X by clicking that window? (Specify ONLY the command without any path or parameters.)

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: /usr/bin/xwininfo -or- xwininfo

Comment

36. Which of the following commands puts the output of the command date into the shell variable mydate?

  • A. mydate=”$(date)”

  • B. mydate=”exec date”

  • C. mydate=”$((date))”

  • D. mydate=”date”

  • E. mydate=”${date}”

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: A

Comment

37. SIMULATION What word is missing from the following SQL statement? insert into tablename ________(909, ‘text’);

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: VALUES -or- values

Comment

38. When the command echo $ outputs 1, which of the following statements is true?

  • A. It is the process ID of the echo command.

  • B. It is the process ID of the current shell.

  • C. It is the exit value of the command executed immediately before echo.

  • D. It is the exit value of the echo command.

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: C

Comment

  • fuck off bitches its echo $?

  • I believe it should be “When the command echo $? outputs 1, which of the following statements is true?”

  • the question is, what does echo $! output

39. A French user has installed the French language pack, but currencies are still being displayed with a leading ‘$’ sign in his spreadsheets. What must be done to fix this?

  • A. Alter the locale.

  • B. Set the timezone correctly.

  • C. Edit /etc/currency.

  • D. Reinstall the French language pack.

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: A

Comment

40. On a system using shadowed passwords, the most correct permissions for /etc/passwd are ___ and the most correct permissions for /etc/shadow are _________.

  • A. -rw-r—–, -r——–

  • B. -rw-r–r–, -r–r–r–

  • C. -rw-r–r–, -r——–

  • D. -rw-r–rw-, -r—–r–

  • E. -rw——-, -r——–

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: C

Comment

41. Which of the following lines is an example of a correct setting for the DISPLAY environment variable?

  • A. hostname:displayname

  • B. hostname:displaynumber

  • C. hostname/displayname

  • D. hostname/displaynumber

  • E. hostname

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: B

Comment

42. You have just set up the X Display Manager as your default display manager. What file should you edit to change the default background for it?

  • A. /etc/X11/xdm/Xsetup

  • B. /etc/X11/prefdm

  • C. /etc/X11/xorg.conf

  • D. /etc/X11/xdm.conf

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: A

Comment

43. What keyword is missing from this code sample of a shell script?

  • A. for B. loop

  • C. until

  • D. while

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: A

Comment

44. Which of the following words is used to restrict the records that are returned from a SELECT query based on a supplied criteria for the values in the records?

  • A. LIMIT

  • B. FROM

  • C. WHERE

  • D. IF

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: C

Comment

45. What output will the command seq 10 produce?

  • A. A continuous stream of numbers increasing in increments of 10 until stopped.

  • B. The numbers 1 through 10 with one number per line.

  • C. The numbers 0 through 9 with one number per line.

  • D. The number 10 to standard output.

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: B

Comment

46. Which file lists which users can execute commands using sudo? (Provide the full path and filename)

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: Answer: /etc/sudoers

Comment

47. What is the command to delete the default gateway from the system IP routing table? (Please specify the complete command with arguments)

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: Answer: route del default, ip route del default, /sbin/route del default, /sbin/ip route del default

Comment

48. Which option, when passed to the gpg command, will enter an interactive menu enabling the user to perform key management related tasks? (Provide only the option)

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: Answer: edit-key, –edit-key

Comment

49. The system’s timezone may be set by linking /etc/localtime to an appropriate file in which directory? (Provide the full path to the directory, without any country information)

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: Answer: /usr/share/zoneinfo/, /usr/share/zoneinfo

Comment

50. Which directory in /etc is used to keep a sample copy of files and directories for when a new user has a home directory created? (Please provide the full path)

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: Answer: /etc/skel, /etc/skel/

Comment

51. Which directory in a user’s home contains configuration files and key rings for GPG?

  • A. ~/gpg.d/

  • B. ~/.gpg/

  • C. ~/.gnupg/

  • D. ~/gnupg/

  • E. ~/.gpg.d/

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: C

Comment

52. When attempting to send a file to another user securely with GPG, which of the following actions must be done?

  • A. Encrypt the file using your public key.

  • B. Encrypt the file using their public key.

  • C. Encrypt the file using your private key.

  • D. Encrypt the file using their private key.

  • E. Sign the file with your public key.

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: B

Comment

53. What command is used to hold keys during a login session to be used for automatic authentication while logging in to other machines using ssh?

  • A. sshd

  • B. ssh-agent

  • C. ssh-keygen

  • D. ssh-add

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: B

Comment

54. Which configuration file would you edit to change default options for outbound ssh sessions?

  • A. /etc/ssh/sshd_config

  • B. /etc/ssh/ssh

  • C. /etc/ssh/client

  • D. /etc/ssh/ssh_config

  • E. /etc/ssh/ssh_client

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: D

Comment

55. Which of the following commands can be used to activate a specific network interface?

  • A. ipup

  • B. ifup

  • C. net

  • D. netup

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: B

Comment

56. Which TWO of the following Class B IPv4 networks are reserved by IANA for private address assignment and private routing?

  • A. 128.0.0.0

  • B. 169.16.0.0

  • C. 169.254.0.0

  • D. 172.16.0.0

  • E. 172.20.0.0

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: DE

Comment

57. The legacy program, provided by CUPS for sending files to the printer queues on the command line is:

  • A. lpd

  • B. lpp

  • C. lpq

  • D. lpr

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: D

Comment

58. Which of the following is NOT a Mail Transport Agent?

  • A. exim

  • B. postfix

  • C. sendmail

  • D. qmail

  • E. mail

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: E

Comment

59. You need to pause the CUPS printer HPLaserjet4, and you want to cancel all print jobs with a message, “hello”. Which command will do this?

  • A. cupsreject -c -r hello HPLaserjet4

  • B. cupsreject -p -m hello HPLaserjet4

  • C. cupsdisable -c -r hello HPLaserjet4

  • D. cupsdisable -p -m hello HPLaserjet4

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: C

Comment

60. What is the main difference between the batch and at commands?

  • A. The batch command will run multiple times.The at command will only run once.

  • B. The batch command will run when system load is low. The at command runs at a specifictime.

  • C. The at command reads commands from standard input. The batch command requires a command line argument.

  • D. The at command e-mails results to the user. The batch command logs results to syslog.

Click to show answer

Correct Answer: B

Comment

    47. 52. When attempting to send a file to another user securely with GPG, which of the following actions must be done?
    48. 53. What command is used to hold keys during a login session to be used for automatic authentication while logging in to other machines using ssh?
    49. 54. Which configuration file would you edit to change default options for outbound ssh sessions?
    50. 55. Which of the following commands can be used to activate a specific network interface?
    51. 56. Which TWO of the following Class B IPv4 networks are reserved by IANA for private address assignment and private routing?
    52. 57. The legacy program, provided by CUPS for sending files to the printer queues on the command line is:
    53. 58. Which of the following is NOT a Mail Transport Agent?
    54. 59. You need to pause the CUPS printer HPLaserjet4, and you want to cancel all print jobs with a message, “hello”. Which command will do this?
    55. 60. What is the main difference between the batch and at commands?
